name: ups-context-sources-all-capped
tags: context cap bounded unbounded memories task decisions body prompt size growth task:251a8531 task_context_summary summarize_task_context first-turn-gated
body:
All UPS-injected system-prompt context sources are bounded, as of task:251a8531: memories (4000-token budget trim, task:3de05199), related_tasks/related_commits/task_rag_chunks (top-3 each, pre-existing), task_context (20-turn SQL LIMIT, pre-existing), mid_task_decisions (last-15 slice, task:251a8531), task_body (3000-char truncation, task:251a8531). task_context_summary (from SummarizeTaskContextNode) was dead code from 2026-06-22 through 2026-07-04, but was re-enabled in task:b9dded3e — it is now live again, gated to fire at most once per task activation (first-turn-only, via len(task_context) <= 1), so its contribution to injected context is bounded by construction (a single ≤200-word bullet summary), not by _enforce_context_budget.

Why: memories was fixed first because it was the obvious uncapped one, but an explicit audit found two more silent unbounded-growth risks (mid_task_decisions grows every tasks__add_decision call over a task's life; task_body has no length cap at all).

How to apply: if a new UPS context-producing node is added, check whether its output list/string is naturally bounded (top-N query, fixed schema) or can grow with session/task lifetime (checkpoint-appended lists, raw DB text) — the latter needs an explicit cap, don't assume it's covered by _enforce_context_budget unless you add it there. If summarize_task_context.py is ever disabled/removed again, update this memory back.
